name: Biscochito Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Granulated sugar, or more to taste
2 tsp Each: fresh lemon juice and brandy
recipeInstructions:
1. Pinch together cinnamon and crushed anise seeds.
2. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
3. Grease an 8-inch round layer cake pan.
4. Stir sour cream and baking soda together; set aside.
5. In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, anise seeds, and salt. Set aside.
6. With an electric mixer on high speed, beat butter, shortening, and brown sugar until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes.
7. Add egg and mix for 1 minute.
8. Lightly fold in sour cream mixture and brandy.
9. Combine the flour mixture with the wet ingredients.
10. Pour batter into prepared pan and smooth the top with a rubber spatula.
11. Stir together granulated sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle evenly over the top of the cake.
12. Bake for 30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
13. While the cake is baking, prepare the peaches by dicing them and mixing with granulated sugar, lemon juice, brandy, cinnamon, and anise. Toss lightly and let stand at least 15 minutes before serving.
14. Allow the cake to cool in the pan for 5 minutes. Loosen the edges of the cake from the sides of the pan with a small knife, then invert onto a serving plate.
15. Serve warm or at room temperature with the peaches. Makes one 8-inch cake.
